evals.name: luxury-landing-page
evals.prompt: We're launching 'Horizon', a luxury minimalist watch brand ($500–$2,000). I need a single-page landing page with a hero section featuring a hero shot, a features grid explaining our craftsmanship, and a product showcase carousel. The vibe should be sophisticated, dark, and editorial — think less Apple, more A.P.C. meets Hodinkee. Please deliver production-ready HTML/CSS with no JS framework dependencies.

expected_output.summary: A complete single-page HTML/CSS landing page for a luxury watch brand with hero, features grid, and product showcase sections, using dark sophisticated aesthetics with gold accents.

accessibility.focus_indicators: visible 2px outline with 4px offset using accent color

performance.font_loading: swap with preconnect and preload for critical fonts
performance.image_optimization: WebP with AVIF fallback, native lazy loading
performance.minimize_reflows: use transform and opacity for animations, GPU-accelerated

responsive.stacking_changes: grid collapses to single column at 768px, reduced font sizes at 480px, navigation becomes hamburger

animation.entrance: fade-in-up with Intersection Observer, stagger children by 100ms
animation.hover: 0.3s ease, accent color shifts, subtle scale(1.02) on cards, underline reveal on links
animation.reduced_motion: disable entrance animations, keep functional transitions

evals.name: fintech-color-palette
evals.prompt: I'm the lead designer at a fintech startup called 'NexusPay'. We're building a B2B payments platform for CFOs and finance directors. I need a color palette that screams trustworthy but doesn't feel like a traditional bank. Modern, confident, tech-forward. Deliver hex codes, HSL values, and explain how each color should be applied across UI surfaces. Bonus points for dark-mode variants.

expected_output.summary: A comprehensive color system for a fintech SaaS with primary, secondary, neutral, semantic, and surface colors including dark mode.

performance.note: N/A — this is a palette recommendation, not rendered output

responsive.note: N/A — palette is responsive by nature

animation.note: N/A — palette recommendation, implementors choose animation

evals.name: ui-component-polish
evals.prompt: Here's my current button and card code — it's functional but looks cheap. Button: <button class='btn btn-primary'>Sign Up</button> / <button class='btn btn-secondary'>Learn More</button> with basic blue/gray styling. Cards are just white boxes with box-shadow: 0 2px 4px. I need you to elevate these: polished hover/active/focus/disabled states, micro-interactions, smooth transitions, modern card with image header, and proper focus rings for accessibility. HTML/CSS only.

expected_output.summary: Polished CSS for button states (hover, active, focus, disabled) and premium card component with image header, shadows, transitions, and accessibility considerations.

performance.note: Use transform and opacity for animations to avoid layout thrashing
performance.transitions: 0.2s ease for colors, 0.3s cubic-bezier for transforms
performance.will-change: use sparingly on hover targets only

responsive.button: full-width on mobile for touch targets

animation.card_hover: box-shadow increases to 0 20px 25px -5px rgba(0,0,0,0.1), translateY(-4px), 0.3s ease

evals.name: typography-pairing
evals.prompt: I'm designing a digital editorial platform called 'Veritas' — a home for long-form investigative journalism, photo essays, and cultural criticism. Think The New Yorker meets A24. I need font pairing recommendations for headings and body text. The feel should be sophisticated, literary, but not stuffy — we want a young, intellectually curious audience. Include sizing scale, weight recommendations, line-height, and practical CSS. Show me a comparison of 3 pairings.

expected_output.summary: Three font pairing options for an editorial platform with complete sizing scales, weight recommendations, line-height, letter-spacing, and usage rationale.

performance.font_loading: swap with preconnect to Google Fonts CDN
performance.subset: latin subset only to reduce payload
performance.variable_fonts: prefer variable fonts over multiple weights where possible

responsive.sizing_adjustments: reduce display/h1 sizes by 30% at mobile (768px breakpoint)
responsive.line_height_increase: slightly increase line-height at narrow widths for readability

animation.note: Typography recommendations don't include animation, but suggest smooth font-loading with FOUT/FOIT handling

evals.name: design-token-system
evals.prompt: We're scaling our design system and need a proper token architecture. We use React 18 + Tailwind CSS 3.x + TypeScript. I need primitive tokens (raw values), semantic tokens (contextual meaning), and component-level tokens. Must support light and dark mode via CSS custom properties. Also include a naming convention strategy and a migration guide from our current tailwind.config.js to the new token system.

expected_output.summary: A complete three-tier design token system (primitive → semantic → component) with CSS custom properties, light/dark mode, Tailwind integration, naming conventions, and migration path.
expected_output.tailwind_integration: Map semantic tokens to Tailwind theme.extend.colors and theme.extend.spacing in tailwind.config.js; use CSS custom properties for dynamic theming

accessibility.note: Token system must enforce contrast ratios at the semantic layer, not primitive layer

performance.note: CSS custom properties have negligible performance impact; use @layer for organized cascade

responsive.note: Responsive tokens handled via Tailwind breakpoint prefixes, not custom properties

animation.note: Define transition-duration and easing tokens as custom properties for consistency

evals.name: analytics-dashboard
evals.prompt: Build me a dark-mode dashboard UI for an analytics platform called 'Pulse'. It needs: a sidebar navigation, a top header with search and notifications, a metrics row (4 KPI cards), a line chart widget, a data table with sortable columns, and a real-time activity feed. Make it feel like Stripe's dashboard meets Linear — clean, data-dense but not overwhelming. HTML/CSS/JS, charts can use Canvas or SVG.

expected_output.summary: A dark-themed analytics dashboard with sidebar, header, KPI cards, line chart, data table, and activity feed, balancing data density with clean typography and visual hierarchy.

accessibility.data_viz: patterns + labels in addition to color for chart elements
accessibility.keyboard_nav: sidebar items focusable, table sortable via keyboard

performance.chart_rendering: use requestAnimationFrame for real-time updates, debounce resize handlers
performance.virtualization: suggest virtual scrolling for data tables with >50 rows
performance.lazy_load: defer non-critical widgets, lazy load activity feed content

responsive.sidebar: collapsible to icon-only at tablet, drawer overlay at mobile
responsive.kpi_grid: 4 columns → 2 columns at tablet → 1 column at mobile
responsive.table: horizontal scroll on mobile, sticky first column

animation.kpi_entrance: counter animation from 0 to value, 1s ease-out
animation.chart_update: smooth transition on data change, 0.5s ease
animation.hover_states: 0.15s ease for row hover, sidebar item highlight
animation.reduced_motion: disable counter animation and entrance effects

evals.name: fitness-mobile-app
evals.prompt: Design the UI for a fitness tracking mobile app called 'Sprint'. Key screens: onboarding (3 slides), home dashboard with today's activity rings, a workout detail screen, and a progress/charts screen. The app should feel energetic but premium — think Nike Training Club meets Strava. Use a mobile-first approach but also describe how it adapts to tablet. Deliver HTML/CSS/JS that looks like a native app (no frameworks).

expected_output.summary: Mobile-first fitness app UI with onboarding, activity dashboard, workout detail, and progress screens, using energetic colors and native-feeling interactions.

accessibility.font_scaling: support Dynamic Type / OS font scaling up to 200%
accessibility.activity_rings: use labels and text alongside color segments

performance.animation_framework: use requestAnimationFrame, avoid setTimeout for animations
performance.image_optimization: responsive images via srcset, WebP preferred

responsive.tablet: activity rings side-by-side, multi-column workout grid, split-view onboarding

animation.activity_rings: circular progress animation, 1.5s ease-out on appear
animation.onboarding: horizontal snap scroll with progress dots, stagger content fade-in

evals.name: ecommerce-product-page
evals.prompt: My brand 'Apotheke' sells premium sustainable skincare — serums, moisturizers, SPF — at $40–$120 per product. The product page needs: hero product image with zoom, multiple variant thumbnails, ingredient accordion, reviews carousel, and add-to-bag with sticky add-to-cart on scroll. The aesthetic should be clean, spa-like, and trustworthy — think Glossier meets Aesop. Deliver HTML/CSS/JS. Make the images placeholder-friendly with gradients.

expected_output.summary: A premium skincare product detail page with image gallery, variant selection, ingredient accordion, reviews, and sticky cart, using a clean spa-like aesthetic.

accessibility.image_zoom: provide alternative text description for zoomed view
accessibility.sticky_cart: ensure it doesn't trap focus, has close mechanism on mobile

performance.image_zoom: use CSS transform: scale() instead of swapping to high-res image until needed
performance.lazy_load: native lazy loading for below-fold images, reviews loaded on interaction
performance.accordion: use content-visibility: auto for off-screen accordion panels
performance.font_display: swap for body, optional for display font

responsive.product_grid: side-by-side (50/50) on desktop, stacked on mobile
responsive.thumbnail_bar: horizontal row on desktop, horizontal scroll on mobile
responsive.sticky_cart: appears at bottom on mobile, inline on desktop
responsive.image_zoom: fullscreen overlay on mobile instead of cursor-follow

animation.image_zoom: smooth scale on hover, follows cursor, 0.2s
animation.add_to_cart: button briefly scales (1.05) then adds item count badge animation
animation.sticky_appear: slide-up from bottom on mobile, 0.3s ease

evals.name: multi-step-registration-form
evals.prompt: We need a multi-step registration form for 'Veridian', a financial wellness app. Steps: 1) email + password, 2) personal info (name, DOB, phone), 3) financial profile (income range, goals), 4) verification (ID upload + selfie). Each step must show proper validation states: idle, typing, valid, invalid with error messages, and success. Also show a loading state during verification. The form should feel trustworthy and calm — not stressful like taxes. HTML/CSS/JS, inline validation, no form libraries.

expected_output.summary: A four-step registration form with real-time inline validation, showing idle, typing, valid, invalid, and loading states for each field, designed to feel calm and trustworthy.

accessibility.error_announcement: use aria-live='polite' region for error messages
accessibility.focus_management: auto-focus first field of each step on forward, focus previous step indicator on back
accessibility.step_indicator: aria-current='step' on active step, screenreader announcements on step change
accessibility.keyboard: Enter advances step from last field, Escape cancels file upload
accessibility.color_not_sole: validation icons and text labels in addition to border colors

performance.file_upload: compress ID images client-side before upload, preview using objectURL
performance.step_transition: pre-render adjacent steps in DOM, toggle visibility

responsive.form: full-width on mobile, centered card on desktop
responsive.steps: vertical stacked on mobile, horizontal indicators on tablet+
responsive.file_upload: camera capture option via accept='image/* capture='environment' on mobile

animation.step_transition: slide-left for next, slide-right for previous, 0.3s ease
animation.validation_icon: fade-in 0.2s, checkmark draws with stroke-dashoffset animation
animation.error_shake: horizontal shake 0.4s on invalid submission attempt
animation.progress_bar: smooth width transition between steps, 0.4s ease
animation.success_animation: step number transforms to checkmark with scale-in 0.3s

evals.name: booking-confirmation-email
evals.prompt: I need an HTML email template for a travel booking platform called 'Drift'. When a customer books a hotel or experience, we send a confirmation email with: booking reference, trip summary (dates, location, accommodation), price breakdown, cancellation policy, and a CTA to 'View Trip Details'. Must render well in Outlook, Gmail, and Apple Mail. I know email CSS is limited — work within those constraints. Make it feel premium, not spammy.

expected_output.summary: A transactional HTML email template for booking confirmation with inline CSS, table-based layout, responsive design for mobile, and email client compatibility notes.

typography.font_family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif

accessibility.alt_text: all images must have descriptive alt text
accessibility.semantic: use <th> for table headers, role='presentation' on layout tables
accessibility.preheader: hidden preheader for screen reader context

performance.inline_css: all styles must be inline — no <style> tags for Outlook compatibility
performance.image_hosting: absolute URLs, use HTTPS, keep under 200KB total
performance.font_loading: use system fonts only, no web fonts (Outlook incompatible)

responsive.approach: mobile-first via @media queries in <style> (Gmail and Apple Mail support)
responsive.stacking: two-column sections collapse to single column, font sizes slightly reduced, button becomes full-width
responsive.outlook: conditional comments for width, VML for background images

animation.note: No animation support in email — use static design only

evals.trigger_condition: Triggers for the visual layout and color aspects, but limited by email CSS constraints. Premium design principles still apply to typography, spacing, and hierarchy.

evals.name: component-library-docs
evals.prompt: We're building an internal component library called 'Primer' and need a documentation site to showcase our components. Design a page layout that includes: sidebar navigation (component list), a main content area with component preview + code snippet tabs, a props/API table, and a dark/light mode toggle. The vibe should be clean and developer-friendly — like Storybook's docs view mixed with Radix Themes. React optional, just give me the HTML/CSS/JS layout.

expected_output.summary: A design system documentation page layout with sidebar navigation, component preview with code tabs, props table, and theme toggle, optimized for developer readability.

accessibility.code_syntax: ensure syntax highlighting has 4.5:1 contrast against code bg
accessibility.skip_link: skip to content link at top of page
accessibility.sidebar: aria-tree or proper nav landmark with aria-label
accessibility.focus_trap: no trap, but manage focus when sidebar toggles on mobile

performance.syntax_highlighting: client-side highlighting with debounced input, or pre-rendered with Prism/Shiki
performance.theme_switch: CSS custom properties switching, no FOUC — load user preference in <head> script
performance.code_blocks: use content-visibility: auto for below-fold code samples

responsive.sidebar: overlay drawer with hamburger on mobile, persistent on desktop
responsive.component_preview: stacked vertically on mobile (preview above code instead of side-by-side)

animation.theme_toggle: smooth transition 0.3s on background-color and color via CSS custom properties
animation.sidebar_open: slide from left 0.3s ease, backdrop fade 0.2s
animation.code_copy: tooltip appears 'Copied!' with fade-in 0.2s, disappears after 2s

evals.name: creative-404-page
evals.prompt: I need a 404 page for a creative agency called 'Studio Mosaic'. They're a colorful, playful brand that does animation, illustration, and web design. The 404 should be fun, not frustrating — maybe an interactive canvas animation, a playful illustration, or a creative layout that showcases their personality while clearly communicating the error. Include a search bar and links back to key sections (Work, About, Contact). HTML/CSS/JS, can use Canvas for the interactive element.

expected_output.summary: A creative, playful 404 error page with interactive canvas animation, clear error messaging, search functionality, and navigation links reflecting the agency's colorful brand.

accessibility.search_label: visible label or aria-label on search input
accessibility.error_announcement: page title updates to '404 - Page Not Found | Studio Mosaic'
accessibility.focus_management: focus moves to main content on navigation from broken link
accessibility.canvas: provide static text description of animation for screen readers

performance.canvas_animation: use requestAnimationFrame, limit to 30fps when not in viewport via Intersection Observer
performance.lazy_assets: defer canvas initialization until page load completes
performance.no_bloat: keep the interactive element lightweight — no heavy libraries

responsive.layout: centered single column, error code shrinks to 4rem on mobile
responsive.canvas: reduce particle count by 50% on mobile for performance
responsive.links: vertical stack on mobile, horizontal row on desktop

animation.canvas_interactive: mouse-tracked particle system or floating shapes in brand colors, 60fps
animation.link_hover: color morph between brand accent colors, 0.3s
animation.page_entrance: staggered fade-in of elements: canvas → error code → message → search → links, each 100ms apart

evals.name: news-skeleton-loading
evals.prompt: I'm building a news aggregator app called 'Current'. The home page loads articles from multiple sources and it can be slow. I need skeleton loading screens for: 1) a featured story card (large hero), 2) a grid of standard article cards, 3) a sidebar with trending topics list, and 4) a detail/article page layout. Show me the HTML/CSS for the skeleton states AND the loaded states. Include shimmer animation and proper content reveal transition. Dark mode too.

expected_output.summary: Skeleton loading components for a news app featuring hero card, article grid, trending sidebar, and article detail page, with shimmer animation and smooth content reveal transitions in both light and dark mode.

typography.skeleton_text_proxy: use same line-height as rendered text, rounded corners via border-radius

accessibility.aria_busy: role='status' aria-busy='true' on skeleton containers, removed on load
accessibility.reduced_motion: prefers-reduced-motion: replace shimmer animation with static opacity pulse at 50%
accessibility.no_flash: never show skeleton after content is rendered (use state management to track loading)

performance.shimmer_animation: use CSS animation with background-position, not JavaScript
performance.gpu_acceleration: use translateZ(0) or will-change on shimmer pseudo-elements
performance.content_visibility: use content-visibility: auto on article cards outside viewport

responsive.hero: full-width on all sizes, but height reduces at mobile
responsive.sidebar: hidden on mobile, revealed at tablet+

animation.content_reveal: fade-in 0.3s ease when skeleton is replaced with content
animation.stagger: skeleton cards stagger appearance by 50ms each for perceived speed

evals.trigger_condition: Triggers for visual design of skeleton components and animations, but may need collaboration with UX skill for loading state architecture and timing logic.

evals.name: travel-hero-video
evals.prompt: Design a hero section for 'Wanderlust', a luxury adventure travel brand. The hero needs a full-viewport background video (aerial drone footage of mountains/coastlines), a headline overlay, a sub-headline, a dual CTA (primary 'Explore Trips' / secondary 'Watch Film'), and a scroll indicator at the bottom. The video should have a dark gradient overlay for text readability. The brand is adventurous but refined — think National Geographic meets Aman Resorts. HTML/CSS/JS video player, graceful fallback for reduced motion and mobile.

expected_output.summary: A full-viewport hero section with background video, dark gradient overlay, headline, dual CTAs, scroll indicator, and graceful fallback for reduced motion and mobile.

accessibility.video_controls: native controls as fallback, autoplay with muted required
accessibility.reduced_motion: prefers-reduced-motion: replace video with static hero image, no autoplay
accessibility.text_readability: ensure 4.5:1 contrast for all text over video overlay
accessibility.skip_video: skip link to jump past hero section to main content
accessibility.no_autoplay_on_mobile: respect mobile browser autoplay restrictions, show static fallback

performance.video_format: MP4 (H.264) with WebM (VP9) fallback, compress to 5Mbps max
performance.video_sizing: reserve aspect-ratio container to prevent CLS, use object-fit: cover
performance.poster_image: provide high-quality JPEG/WebP poster that loads before video
performance.lazy_load: defer video loading with preload='metadata', use Intersection Observer to start playback
performance.mobile_fallback: serve static image instead of video on slow connections (detect via Network Information API or connection type)

responsive.headline: 4.5rem → 3rem (tablet) → 2rem (mobile)
responsive.ctas: side-by-side on desktop, stacked full-width on mobile, full-width touch targets (min 48px)
responsive.video: video continues full-width on tablet, static image on mobile (battery/data conservation)

animation.video_transition: crossfade between poster image and video playback, 0.5s
animation.text_entrance: staggered: headline fades in (0.6s), then subheadline (0.8s), then CTAs (1s), then scroll indicator (1.2s)
animation.cta_hover: primary: background darkens 0.2s, secondary: border fills with background 0.3s
animation.parallax: optional subtle parallax on video or overlay as user scrolls away, 0.5x speed

evals.name: saas-navigation-mega-menu
evals.prompt: We're redesigning the navigation for 'Forge', a developer tools SaaS (CI/CD, monitoring, logging). The current nav is a flat list of 12 links — it's overwhelming. We need a mega-menu navigation with: 1) Main nav bar with logo + product name, 2) 4 dropdown mega-menus (Products, Solutions, Developers, Pricing), 3) Each mega-menu has columns with icon + heading + description links, 4) A featured section (e.g. 'New: AI-powered debugging'), 5) CTA buttons 'Start Free Trial' + 'Sign In'. Must be keyboard accessible and responsive. HTML/CSS/JS.

expected_output.summary: A responsive mega-menu navigation for a developer tools SaaS with four dropdowns, multi-column layouts, featured content sections, CTAs, and full keyboard accessibility.

accessibility.keyboard: Escape closes dropdown, ArrowDown/Up navigates items, Enter/ Space activates, ArrowRight/Left switches dropdowns
accessibility.aria: aria-expanded on nav buttons, aria-controls links dropdown, role='navigation' with aria-label, role='menubar' / 'menuitem' pattern
accessibility.focus_trap: focus is trapped within open dropdown, returning to trigger on close
accessibility.hover_behavior: 300ms open delay to prevent accidental opens, 300ms close delay with keep-open-on-hover-menu
accessibility.touch: tap outside closes dropdown, first tap opens (no hover on mobile)

performance.lazy_dropdown: render dropdown content only when triggered (or pre-render hidden with visibility for instant open)
performance.icon_sprites: inline SVG icons or sprite sheet, no icon library bundle
performance.event_listeners: delegate events to nav container, not individual items

responsive.tablet: mega-menu collapses to single-column stacked layout within dropdown, remove featured section
responsive.mobile: full hamburger drawer with accordion-style expandable sections, CTAs at bottom of drawer
responsive.touch_targets: minimum 44px height for all mobile nav items

animation.dropdown_open: fade-in + translateY(-8px) → translateY(0), 0.2s ease, with opacity transition
